items: Sign out button is now directly visible in the sidebar footer — no longer hidden inside a dropdown menu
Collapsed sidebar icons — logo, user avatar, and sign-out now match the size and alignment of all navigation icons
User avatar shows a tooltip with your name when hovering in collapsed sidebar mode
Logo no longer clips on the right edge when the sidebar is collapsed
Knowledge Graph layout — right-side buttons (Fit to Screen, Analyze posts) are now fully visible
Sidebar navigation links work correctly as SPA routes without full page reloads
Mobile sidebar auto-closes after selecting a navigation item
Collapsible sidebar navigation — a shared left sidebar replaces per-page header navigation across all authenticated pages
Sidebar collapses to an icon rail on desktop and becomes a slide-out sheet on mobile
Dashboard page placeholder — reserved for future analytics and insights
Library page — the main content view (formerly Dashboard) now focuses purely on content actions
Consistent app shell — every page now shares the same layout with sidebar, top bar, and user menu
Cleaner Library header — navigation buttons removed; only action buttons remain (Export, Bulk Import, Enrich All, Add Content)
Sidebar highlights the active page and supports keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd+B) to toggle
Post enrichment — scrape original URLs to fill in missing images, author info, and fuller content for bulk-imported posts
Per-post Enrich button appears on cards and list items that are missing data (image, author name, or author URL)
Enrich All button on the Dashboard processes every unenriched post in one click, with progress feedback
Mobile-friendly — Enrich All also available in the mobile more menu
SSRF protection — URL validation with protocol allowlisting (http/https only) and private IP blocking for all scrape endpoints
Per-hop redirect validation — redirects are now followed manually with each hop checked against the blocklist before fetching
IPv6-mapped IPv4 blocking — addresses like ::ffff:127.0.0.1 are correctly identified and blocked
DNS resolution check — hostnames that resolve to private network addresses are rejected
Dashboard header decluttered — navigation buttons (Knowledge Graph, API Docs, API Keys, Extension, Export) converted to compact icon-only circular buttons with tooltips
Better error messages — backend error details now surface properly in toast notifications instead of generic status text
LinkedIn CSV bulk import — parser rewritten to correctly handle multi-line quoted fields and map LinkedIn's export column headers (Post Text, Post Link, Profile)
Mobile-responsive overhaul — the entire app is now fully usable on phones and small tablets
Dashboard action buttons collapse into a compact "More" menu on mobile, keeping the screen uncluttered
Mobile search — tap the search icon in the header to reveal a full-width search bar on small screens
Tag filter bar scrolls horizontally on mobile instead of wrapping into multiple rows
Knowledge Graph entity details open as a bottom sheet on mobile instead of a fixed-width sidebar
Knowledge Graph controls (search, filters, action buttons) adapt to narrow screens without overlapping
Landing page features stack vertically on mobile with readable text sizes and proper spacing
Post cards and list items — delete buttons always visible on mobile (no hover required on touch devices), thumbnails hidden on very small screens to prevent text squeeze
All secondary pages (API Docs, API Keys, Extension, Changelog) refined for mobile spacing, touch targets, and readability
Knowledge Graph — interactive force-directed visualization of entities (people, companies, topics, technologies) extracted from your saved posts
AI entity extraction — OpenAI-powered pipeline automatically identifies and links entities when you save a post
Entity detail panel — click any node to see connected entities, related posts, and descriptions
Backfill analyzer — process all existing posts to build your knowledge graph retroactively
Search and filter — search entities by name and filter by type (people, companies, topics, technologies)
Export to CSV — download all your saved posts as a spreadsheet-ready CSV file
Export to Markdown — download your posts as a beautifully formatted Markdown document
Filter-aware export — exports respect your current filters (favorites, tags, collections, platform) so you export exactly what you see
Dark mode — toggle between light and dark themes from any page header, with your preference saved automatically
Favorites filter — quickly view only your favorited posts using the Favorites tab on the dashboard
Dark mode compatibility — fixed all hardcoded white backgrounds across the app to adapt to dark theme
MCP Server — AI agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor) can now query your superBrain knowledge base via the Model Context Protocol
5 MCP tools — search_knowledge, list_posts, save_post, scrape_url, and list_collections available as agent-callable tools
MCP setup docs — Developers page now includes setup instructions for Claude Desktop and Cursor with config examples
Unified roadmap in README — merged Free and Pro tiers into a single feature table
Public REST API (v1) — 7 endpoints for posts, search, collections, and scraping at /api/v1/*
API key management — generate, copy, and revoke API keys from the new API Keys page
Bearer token authentication — secure your API requests with per-key tokens
Rate limiting — 100 requests per minute per API key with standard X-RateLimit headers
API Documentation page — interactive docs with curl examples for every endpoint
Developers page navigation — API Docs and API Keys buttons added to the dashboard, with cross-links between pages
API docs show dynamic base URL — curl examples automatically use the correct domain for your deployment
Platform filtering in API — filter posts by platform (linkedin, substack, other) via the public API
GitHub sync script now properly excludes attached_assets/ directory
Full-text keyword search — search bar now matches across content, summary, author, tags, URL, and platform
Collapsible AI sidebar — smoothly slide the AI Assistant panel open and closed with a spring animation
Open-source release — superBrain is now available on GitHub under the MIT license with full setup docs
Debounced search input — search waits 300ms after you stop typing for a snappier experience
Better toggle icons — AI sidebar button now shows contextual icons (sparkles when closed, panel-close when open)
Tags on posts — add tags when creating a post using Enter or comma to separate, displayed as badges on cards and list items
Tag filtering — clickable tag bar on the dashboard lets you filter posts by any tag in one click
Collections — create named collections to organize your saved posts into groups
Add to Collection — use the folder icon on any post card to add or remove it from your collections
Collection filtering — select a collection from the Collections menu to view only posts in that group
Dashboard now combines tab, tag, and collection filters so you can drill down to exactly the posts you need
Real-time UI updates when adding or removing posts from collections — no page refresh needed
Dynamic typewriter effect on landing page hero — cycles through "LinkedIn" and "Substack" to showcase multi-platform support
Substack support — scrape and save Substack articles and notes with platform-specific content extraction
Custom-domain Substack detection — automatically identifies Substack sites that use custom domains (e.g., lennysnewsletter.com) by inspecting page HTML
Platform-specific badge colors — LinkedIn (blue), Substack (orange), Other (gray) with dark mode support
Smarter author extraction for Substack — filters out "Substack" as author, parses note profiles (@username format), and falls back to og:title parsing
LinkedIn content cleaning — prefers clean og:description over raw page text to prevent author bios from appearing in post content
Grid and list view toggle on dashboard — switch between card grid and compact list layouts
List view component with expandable content, inline metadata, and thumbnail previews
Content cleaning pipeline — multi-stage processing strips author bios, social engagement metadata (likes, comments, share buttons), and normalizes whitespace
Author extraction improvements — properly parses LinkedIn og:title pipe-delimited format and uses multiple fallback strategies
URL auto-scraping — paste a URL and automatically extract post content, images, and author info
Scrape preview in create dialog — "Fetch" button fills in content, image, and author fields before saving
Bulk import — upload CSV or JSON files to import multiple posts at once
Chrome extension — save LinkedIn posts directly from your feed with one click
Initial release of superBrain — your second brain for LinkedIn content
Save, organize, and search posts with AI-powered semantic search
Manual post creation with tags, author info, and URL tracking
AI search sidebar — ask natural language questions about your saved content
Favorite posts for quick access
Authentication via Replit sign-in
